notes.anchor: What I wanted done I wrote to Pomponius
notes.note: Pomponius is Titus Pomponius Atticus, Cicero's lifelong friend and financial agent. The deliberately guarded wording (Cicero will not write openly because he has already instructed Atticus) reflects his caution at Brundisium in 47 BC about the family's money and Tullia's dowry.

notes.anchor: The seventh day before the Ides of Quintilis
notes.note: Quintilis was the old name for the month later renamed July in Caesar's honour; the Ides fell on the 13th, so this dates to 9 July 47 BC by inclusive reckoning.
